verification_notes: 27-hole Seve Ballesteros design confirmed by multiple sources including golfasian.com, golfsavers.com, golftripz.com, and official site mountaincreekthailand.com. Opened 2011 per golfasian.com. 27 holes at par 72/36 (7,615 yards total) from golfasian.com. Address confirmed as 99/9 Moo 12, Mittraphap Road KM91, Tambol Lat Bua Khao, Amphur Sikhio, Nakhon Ratchasima 30340. Caddie and cart mandatory per thailandgolfers.com. Green fee range 1,100–2,500 THB per round from golfsavers.com — likely reflects different course combinations (Highland/Creek/Valley) and day type, not a clean weekday/weekend split; set to null with notes. Distance ~170 km from Bangkok, ~2 hrs confirmed by golfasian.com and multiple booking platforms. GPS is model estimate for Lat Bua Khao / Sikhio area. Phone confirmed from official site and multiple sources. priceRange note for Stage 5: ฿฿ (caddie+cart bundled total likely 2,500–3,500 THB all-in per round).
disputes: Green fee pricing not clearly split weekday vs weekend on any source reviewed. Golfsavers shows 1,100–2,500 THB range which likely reflects the three different 9-hole combinations and day types. Officialwebsite lists coupon prices (700 THB / 18 holes) but these are discounted member/promotion rates. Setting weekday/weekend to null as no clean itemised split was found; total all-in (GF+caddie+cart) is the booking unit.
Year opened: golfasian.com states 2011; some sources are silent on opening year. 2011 accepted from golfasian.com as best available source.

prose.overview: Mountain Creek Golf Resort is the only golf course in Thailand designed by Seve Ballesteros, the two-time Masters champion and five-time Open Championship winner. Located at Sikhio in Nakhon Ratchasima Province, past the gateway of Khao Yai National Park, the resort opened in 2011 with a 27-hole complex built around the dramatic terrain of the Lam Takhong reservoir watershed. Ballesteros divided the layout into three named nine-hole loops — Highlands, Creek, and Valley — each reflecting the natural character of its setting. The resort operates as a full-service golf destination with accommodation, spa, restaurant, and practice facilities on site. Both caddies and carts are mandatory and are bundled into round pricing, which simplifies the booking experience. The course attracts golfers seeking a signature-architect layout with genuine elevation change and forest scenery — a significant departure from the flat delta courses of the Bangkok region.
prose.layout_and_experience: The 27-hole complex offers three distinct nine-hole combinations, all playable as standard 18-hole rounds. Total yardage across all 27 holes reaches 7,615 yards from the back tees, indicating a genuine championship test. The Highlands nine runs across the elevated ridges of the property with panoramic views over the Lam Takhong reservoir and surrounding forested hills. The Creek nine is defined by its eponymous water feature, threading through riparian corridors with tight approach angles and premium on accuracy. The Valley nine descends into lower-lying terrain with the widest fairways and most accessible scoring opportunities of the three loops, making it a popular anchor for first-time visitors. Ballesteros's signature design element — a large S-shaped bunker — appears on hole three, providing a visual trademark. Carts are mandatory due to the property's hilly terrain; the elevation changes that make the course visually dramatic would render walking impractical for most visitors. A driving range and practice facilities are on site for pre-round preparation.
prose.tips: Book any combination of the three nine-hole loops — Highlands/Creek, Creek/Valley, and Highlands/Valley — depending on your skill level and appetite for challenge. First-time visitors typically start with Valley and Creek, saving the more exposed Highlands nine for a return round when the layout is familiar. The course is approximately 170 km northeast of Bangkok via Mittraphap Highway (Route 2) — allow at least two and a half hours including roadside stops. The resort sits above 500 metres altitude, meaning temperatures are consistently 5–8°C cooler than Bangkok — a major advantage during the March–May hot season. November through February offers ideal conditions: cool air, dry fairways, and uncrowded tee sheets on weekdays. Book via the official website or a booking agent at least one day in advance on weekdays and two days in advance on weekends.
prose.location_and_access: Mountain Creek Golf Resort is located at 99/9 Moo 12, Mittraphap Road KM 91, Tambol Lat Bua Khao, Amphur Sikhio, Nakhon Ratchasima 30340, approximately 170 km northeast of central Bangkok. The drive takes approximately two hours and fifteen minutes via Route 2 (Mittraphap Highway) northbound toward Korat. The resort sits past the Khao Yai National Park entrance, roughly 30 minutes beyond Pak Chong town. A private car or organised transfer is required; the resort itself can arrange airport and Bangkok hotel transfers on request.
prose.rental_cta_context: Heading up to Mountain Creek for a Khao Yai golf weekend? LENGOLF delivers TaylorMade and Callaway sets to your Bangkok hotel the evening before you drive out, so you arrive ready to play.
